steps.title: Find the ice cream calcium
steps.content:
The batch uses $3$ cups of ice cream, with $242\,\text{mg}$ of calcium per cup:
$$3(242)=726$$
So the ice cream contributes $726\,\text{mg}$ of calcium.

steps.title: Subtract to isolate bananas
steps.content:
The whole batch has $738\,\text{mg}$, so the $2$ bananas contribute:
$$738-726=12$$

steps.title: Divide by two bananas
steps.content:
Each banana has half of that calcium:
$$\frac{12}{2}=6$$
The answer is A.
